reasoning: The market estimate on Aug 21, 2025 was 10%. Since then, there is no evidence of actual legal formation of the party. The key hurdles are both lack of interest and legal/bureaucratic roadblocks. Musk's initial announcement on July 5, 2025 seems to have been mostly an emotional reaction to Trump's spending bill. While he formally declared on X that the party was "formed," the resolution criteria requires "filing the necessary paperwork with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) to establish the party's legal status." By late August 2025, reports indicated that he was "quietly putting the brakes" on the idea. Although Musk tweeted a generic denial ("Nothing @WSJ says should ever be thought of as true"), he hasn't provided any evidence of formal action. Further complicating matters, the FEC lost its quorum in May 2025 and, as of reporting in July, would be unable to adjudicate or officially approve a new national political party until new commissioners are nominated by Trump—who the party is meant to challenge. Finally, running a new political entity requires a massive logistical undertaking, and given Musk's reconciliation efforts/business needs (SpaceX, Tesla), turning a momentary political outburst into a legally registered party by late 2025 is highly improbable. My estimate is 4%, reflecting the residual chance that escalating feuds with the administration provoke him to instruct lawyers to just file the paperwork as a protest.
